metadata.summary: Deploy a static site to Cloudflare Pages from a GitHub repo: build settings, env vars, custom domain, preview-per-PR, _headers, _redirects, Functions, Wrangler local dev.

content.title: Cloudflare Pages from a GitHub Repo
content.summary: Connect a GitHub repository to Cloudflare Pages so every push to the production branch publishes to the global CDN and every pull request gets its own preview URL. Configure framework preset, build command, output directory, environment variables (split production vs preview), custom domain with SSL Full Strict, `_headers` and `_redirects` files, optional Pages Functions for edge logic, and Wrangler CLI for local parity.

why.p: Cloudflare Pages gives a zero-cost global CDN, automatic HTTPS, unlimited requests on the free tier, and preview-per-pull-request out of the box. Connecting from GitHub puts deploys on the developer-already-uses-it path: push to `main` ⇒ production, open a PR ⇒ unique preview URL. Wrangler keeps local dev identical to the edge runtime so Functions behave the same in development and production.

list.item: Astro / Next.js (static export) / Gatsby / Hugo / Vite SPA / SvelteKit static repo on GitHub.
You want a global CDN with a generous free tier (unlimited bandwidth, 500 builds/month).
You want automatic preview-per-PR with a unique URL on `*.pages.dev`.
You need edge handlers for redirects, A/B tests, geo-routing, or lightweight APIs (Pages Functions).
You serve a custom domain with managed SSL and want CF-Cache-Status on every response.
Heavy server-side runtime that needs a custom Node version not on the Pages-supported matrix — use a regular VPS or Workers.
Monorepo where the build takes longer than 20 minutes — Pages caps build time; switch to GitHub Actions + Wrangler direct deploy.
Traffic that exceeds the free tier without a billing plan — switch to paid plan or migrate to Workers + R2.
Server-rendered Next.js without the `@cloudflare/next-on-pages` adapter — runtime errors at the edge.
Apps that need long-running background jobs — Functions hit the 30-second CPU limit; use Workers Queues / Cron Triggers.

how-to.step: Prerequisites: a Cloudflare account (free is enough), a GitHub repo with a working static-site project, Node 18+ locally, and the Wrangler CLI installed (`npm i -g wrangler` or use `npx wrangler`).

Create the Pages project. Either via dashboard (Workers and Pages → Create → Pages → Connect to Git) or CLI: `wrangler login` then `wrangler pages project create my-static-site --production-branch main`.

Authorize the Cloudflare GitHub App on the target repo. Choose between "All repositories" (simpler) or "Only select repositories" (recommended for accounts with private code). The app must have Read access to code, metadata, and deployments.

Select the repo and the production branch (usually `main` or `master`). Pages will list every other branch as a candidate for preview deployments — leave the default "All non-production branches" unless you want to restrict it.

Set build settings. Pick a Framework preset (Astro / Next.js / Gatsby / Hugo / Vite / None). Confirm Build command (`npm run build`), Build output directory (Astro: `dist`, Gatsby: `public`, Next-static: `out`, Vite: `dist`, Hugo: `public`), and Root directory (leave empty for single-app repos; set to `apps/web/` for monorepos).

Configure environment variables. Add separate Production and Preview sets in the dashboard (Settings → Environment variables). Public values (`PUBLIC_API_URL`, `NODE_VERSION`) go as plain vars; tokens (`CF_API_TOKEN`, `SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN`) go as Secrets — never commit them to the repo.

Attach a custom domain. Settings → Custom domains → Set up a custom domain → enter `example.com`. If the domain is on Cloudflare DNS, the CNAME flatten happens automatically; otherwise add a CNAME from `example.com` to `<project>.pages.dev` at your DNS provider. Set SSL/TLS mode to Full (Strict) on the zone.

Add `_redirects` and `_headers` files at the build-output root for client-side routing fallback (`/* /index.html 200`) and CSP / cache-control headers. Pages reads them automatically — no extra config.

Optionally add a `functions/` directory at the repo root (or under `pages_build_output_dir`/`functions/`) for edge handlers. Each `.ts`/`.js` file becomes a route. Keep them under the 30-second CPU budget.
Run `wrangler pages dev <output-dir>` locally to serve the built site with Pages-compatible Functions. Use `npm run build && wrangler pages dev dist` to mirror production behavior.
Commit, push to a non-production branch, open a PR. Cloudflare comments the preview URL on the PR within seconds of build completion. Merging to `main` triggers a production deploy.

Verify with `wrangler pages deployment list --project-name my-static-site`, `curl -I https://my-static-site.pages.dev`, and a browser check on the custom domain. Inspect `Cache-Control` and `CF-Cache-Status` headers; tail logs with `wrangler pages deployment tail`.

warning.p: Never commit API tokens, secrets, or `.env*` files to the repo before linking it to Pages. The first build streams the working tree into the build container and prints file paths — leaked credentials show up in build logs that are world-readable on public projects. Rotate any value that ever lived in a tracked file.

commands.command: wrangler pages project create my-static-site --production-branch main
gh repo clone faionfaion/my-static-site && cd my-static-site
npm run build && wrangler pages dev dist
wrangler pages deploy dist --project-name my-static-site --branch main
wrangler pages secret put SENTRY_AUTH_TOKEN --project-name my-static-site

template.purpose: Minimal Pages config: production+preview env vars, NODE_VERSION pin, pages_build_output_dir, optional KV/D1 bindings.

content.prompt:
You are a Cloudflare Pages config generator. Produce a complete starter set for a project that is being connected to Cloudflare Pages from GitHub.
INPUT (provided by caller, fill in placeholders): framework: <astro | next-static | gatsby | hugo | vite | sveltekit-static> project_name: <kebab-case slug, must be unique per Cloudflare account> production_branch: <main | master | production> custom_domain: <example.com or empty if pages.dev only> api_url_prod: <https://api.example.com> api_url_preview: <https://api-staging.example.com or same as prod> node_version: <18 | 20 | 22>
OUTPUT — emit exactly three files, each in a separate fenced code block, using the framework's conventional output directory: - astro=dist, next-static=out, gatsby=public, hugo=public, vite=dist, sveltekit-static=build
1) wrangler.toml Required keys: name, compatibility_date (today's ISO date), pages_build_output_dir, [env.production.vars] block with NODE_VERSION and PUBLIC_API_URL=<api_url_prod>, [env.preview.vars] block with PUBLIC_API_URL=<api_url_preview>. Comment out KV/D1 bindings unless asked.
2) <output-dir>/_redirects - SPA fallback rule: `/* /index.html 200` (skip for Astro/Hugo unless project is SPA). - One example 301 from a legacy path to a new path.
3) <output-dir>/_headers - `/*` block with security defaults: Strict-Transport-Security (max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains; preload), X-Content-Type-Options nosniff, Referrer-Policy strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Permissions-Policy camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(), and a starter Content-Security-Policy with `default-src 'self'` plus the custom_domain origin. - `/assets/*` block with Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable.
CONSTRAINTS - Do not invent secrets in plaintext; show them only as commented-out CLI hints (`wrangler pages secret put NAME ...`). - Always include both production and preview env blocks even when values are identical, so the team can diverge later without restructuring. - Use the latest Wrangler v3 syntax. No deprecated `[site]` or `[build.upload]` keys.
Return only the three code blocks, no prose.

example.title: Astro repo connected to Pages with custom domain and preview-per-PR
example.description: Static Astro site, monolithic repo, GitHub-connected Pages project, custom apex domain on Cloudflare DNS, preview deploys for every PR.
example.code: Repo: github.com/acme/marketing-site (Astro 5) Project name: acme-marketing Branch (prod): main Build command: npm run build Output dir: dist Root dir: (empty) Env vars: Production: NODE_VERSION=20 PUBLIC_API_URL=https://api.acme.com Preview: NODE_VERSION=20 PUBLIC_API_URL=https://api-staging.acme.com Custom domain: acme.com (apex) + www.acme.com (CNAME flatten via Cloudflare DNS) SSL/TLS mode: Full (Strict) Preview: Enabled for all non-production branches Result: - main push → https://acme.com (and https://acme-marketing.pages.dev) - PR open → https://<commit-sha>.acme-marketing.pages.dev (auto-comment on PR) - Build time → ~45s (cold), ~12s (cached) - Edge cache → CF-Cache-Status: HIT after first request per POP
example.note: The PR-preview URL is stable per commit, so QA can link the exact build under review. Merging the PR triggers production within ~30 seconds.

example.title: Server-rendered Next.js without the Pages adapter
example.description: Connecting a Next.js app that uses `getServerSideProps`, route handlers, or `next/image` with default loader to Pages without the `@cloudflare/next-on-pages` adapter.
example.code:
Repo: github.com/acme/dashboard (Next.js 14, app router, SSR) Build command: npm run build Output dir: .next <-- WRONG; Pages cannot serve raw .next/ Result: - Build "succeeds" (npm run build exits 0) - First request to https://acme-dashboard.pages.dev → 522 / blank - Edge logs: "missing _worker.js" and "no static assets at /"
Fix path: npm install --save-dev @cloudflare/next-on-pages Build command: npx @cloudflare/next-on-pages@latest Output dir: .vercel/output/static Add: nodejs_compat compatibility flag (Settings → Functions)
example.note: Plain `next build` produces a Node-only artifact. Pages Functions run on Workerd (V8 isolates), not Node — the adapter rewrites the build into a `_worker.js` plus static assets that the edge can serve.

antipattern.description: Using Pages Functions for long-running tasks (PDF rendering, video transcode, multi-step LLM calls) that exceed 30 seconds of CPU time.
antipattern.reason: Pages Functions inherit the Workers CPU/wall-time limits. Long jobs hit the limit, return 524, and waste a billed invocation. Move the work to Workers Queues (`wrangler queues create`) with a producer Function that enqueues a job and returns 202, then a consumer Worker that processes in the background.

antipattern.description: Storing the same env value as both a plain Variable and a Secret in the dashboard, then editing only the Variable.
antipattern.reason: Pages merges Variables and Secrets at runtime; the Secret wins on collision. After editing only the Variable the deploy continues to use the stale Secret value, producing a silent config drift that is hard to debug. Always store each value in exactly one of the two.

checklist.check: GitHub App authorized on the exact repo (or org with repo access), with Read on code + metadata + deployments.
Build command runs locally without warnings: `rm -rf node_modules && npm ci && npm run build`.
Output directory matches the framework default (Astro=dist, Gatsby=public, Next-static=out, Vite=dist, Hugo=public).
Environment variables defined for BOTH Production and Preview; values diverge where staging differs from prod.
No secrets committed to the repo; tokens stored as Pages Secrets via dashboard or `wrangler pages secret put`.
NODE_VERSION pinned via env var (matches the version used in CI / `engines.node` in package.json).
Custom domain DNS: CNAME from apex/www to `<project>.pages.dev` (or use Cloudflare-managed zone for automatic flattening).
SSL/TLS mode set to Full (Strict) on the Cloudflare zone; "Always Use HTTPS" enabled.
Preview branch protection: production branch is protected on GitHub (required PR + review) so prod deploys are gated.
`_redirects` rules cover SPA fallback (if SPA) and any legacy path 301s.
`_headers` defines a strict CSP, HSTS, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, and `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` on `/assets/*`.
Build logs reviewed for warnings (deprecated APIs, unused vars). Build time under the 20-minute limit.
First production deploy verified with `curl -I` against both `*.pages.dev` and the custom domain. Status 200, expected headers present.

verify.step: Run `wrangler pages deployment list --project-name my-static-site` — last entry must be marked "Success" with a recent timestamp and the correct commit SHA.
HEAD probe on the pages.dev URL: `curl -I https://my-static-site.pages.dev` — expect HTTP/2 200, `cf-cache-status` header present (MISS on first hit, HIT on second), `content-type: text/html`.
HEAD probe on the custom domain: `curl -I https://example.com` — expect 200 + `strict-transport-security` set + `cf-ray` header proving the response came from Cloudflare's edge.

Validate caching: a second `curl -I` within 60s on the same path must return `cf-cache-status: HIT`. If it stays MISS, check the `Cache-Control` directive in `_headers` and the file's path (HTML pages are cached only with explicit max-age).
Tail live build/runtime logs: `wrangler pages deployment tail --project-name my-static-site` — push a trivial commit and watch the build proceed; no red error lines should appear.

Open `https://my-static-site.pages.dev` and the custom domain in a browser; load DevTools → Network tab → confirm `cf-ray`, `cf-cache-status`, expected CSP. Lighthouse should report HTTPS + valid certificate.

Open a draft PR on a feature branch — within ~60s the Cloudflare GitHub bot must post a preview URL comment of the form `https://<sha-prefix>.my-static-site.pages.dev`. Click through and confirm the build matches the PR head.
